repositories.citation: [1] Polymarket. 'py-clob-client: Python client for the Polymarket CLOB.' GitHub, Updated Jan 13 2026. https://github.com/Polymarket/py-clob-client
key_features: Read-only market data access without authentication
Full trading capabilities with wallet integration
Support for both EOA and proxy wallets
repositories.architecture: REST API client with WebSocket support, Polygon blockchain integration for settlements
repositories.usage_example:
from py_clob_client.client import ClobClient
client = ClobClient('https://clob.polymarket.com')
markets = client.get_simplified_markets()
mid_price = client.get_midpoint('<token-id>')
limitations: Complex wallet setup for trading (USDC/Conditional Token approvals)
Historical data API has reported blank response issues (Issue #189)

repositories.citation: [2] lowgrind. 'kalshi-python: The official API client library for kalshi.com.' GitHub. https://github.com/lowgrind/kalshi-python
key_features: Order book data for all markets
repositories.architecture: REST API with OpenAPI spec, USD settlements (no crypto), tier-based rate limiting
repositories.usage_example:
pip install kalshi-python
# See docs.kalshi.com for authentication and usage examples
limitations: Prime tier requires 7.5% of exchange traded volume
Rate limits make true HFT impractical
alternatives: kalshi-py (type-safe client built from OpenAPI spec)

repositories.citation: [3] bcongdon. 'PyManifold: Python API client for Manifold Markets.' GitHub. https://github.com/bcongdon/PyManifold
key_features: No authentication required for read operations
repositories.architecture: REST API wrapper, play money settlements, community-driven platform
repositories.usage_example:
from pymanifold import ManifoldClient
client = ManifoldClient()
markets = client.list_markets()
client.create_bet(contractId, amount, outcome)
limitations: Not published to PyPI yet (installation via source)

repositories.citation: [4] tuttlepower. 'predictit_markets: Simple Python code to retrieve PredictIt market data.' GitHub. https://github.com/tuttlepower/predictit_markets
key_features: Returns Pandas DataFrame of market data
repositories.architecture: Simple wrapper around PredictIt public REST API
repositories.usage_example:
# Returns Pandas DataFrame with market data
# Endpoint: https://www.predictit.org/api/marketdata/all/
limitations: API only shows OPEN markets (no closed markets)
Historical data requires manual CSV download per contract
alternatives: Manual CSV downloads from PredictIt website
Academic data sharing program for researchers

repositories.citation: [5] Metaculus. 'forecasting-tools: Framework for building AI Forecasting Bots.' GitHub, Updated Jan 13 2026. https://github.com/Metaculus/forecasting-tools
key_features: Supports Binary, Multiple Choice, Numeric, and Date questions
Historical forecast data with timestamp filtering
repositories.architecture: Python framework with API wrapper, forecasting aggregation engine, community-driven predictions
repositories.usage_example:
# Create METACULUS_TOKEN at https://metaculus.com/aib
# API docs: https://www.metaculus.com/api/
# Framework provides bot templates for automated forecasting
limitations: Focused on forecasting/prediction rather than trading

repositories.citation: [6] warproxxx. 'poly_data: Polymarket Data Retriever.' GitHub. https://github.com/warproxxx/poly_data
repositories.architecture: Data scraper/processor with CSV export capabilities
repositories.usage_example:
# Fetches and structures Polymarket data
# Exports: markets.csv, orderFilled.csv, trades.csv

repositories.citation: [7] arbbets. 'Prediction-Markets-Data: Python script for multi-platform data collection.' GitHub. https://github.com/arbbets/Prediction-Markets-Data
key_features: Years of historical prediction market data
repositories.architecture: Unified data collection framework across multiple prediction market platforms
repositories.usage_example:
# Unified interface for multiple prediction markets
# Supports historical data analysis

common_patterns: REST API as primary interface across all platforms
WebSocket support for real-time data (Polymarket, Kalshi)
Token/API key authentication with varying expiration
Rate limiting on free tiers (100 req/min typical)
Historical data via dedicated endpoints or CSV downloads
Pandas DataFrame integration in Python libraries
best_practices: Use official clients where available (py-clob-client, kalshi-python)
Implement proper error handling for rate limits (HTTP 429)
Cache historical data locally to minimize API calls
Use WebSocket connections for real-time price updates
Store timestamps as Unix time for cross-platform consistency
Implement exponential backoff for failed requests
pitfalls: PredictIt API only shows OPEN markets (no closed markets in API)
Kalshi tokens expire every 30 minutes (requires re-auth)
Polymarket requires blockchain wallet setup for trading
Rate limits can be restrictive for high-frequency strategies
Historical data availability varies significantly by platform
Regulatory uncertainty for sports markets (state vs federal jurisdiction)
emerging_trends: CFTC regulation vs state gambling law conflicts
AI forecasting bots becoming mainstream (Metaculus tournaments)
Lower vig/juice on prediction markets vs traditional books
Migration of sharp bettors to prediction markets for better pricing
Integration with DeFi protocols (Polymarket on Polygon)

implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Polymarket py-clob-client with WebSocket for NFL/NBA/MLB markets
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Most comprehensive sports coverage, real-time updates, no authentication needed for read-only, active maintenance
Only CFTC-regulated option, USD settlements, legal clarity for US users, growing sports market coverage

implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Polymarket poly_data + py-clob-client for historical prices
implementation_recommendations.rationale: CSV exports for bulk analysis, /prices-history endpoint for time series, extensive sports market history

implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Custom integration using Prediction-Markets-Data framework or individual platform clients
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Need to compare Kalshi vs Polymarket pricing, unified data structure, historical comparison capability
Play money platform, low stakes testing, active community, Kelly calculator included
Aggregated crowd wisdom, historical forecast accuracy data, AI bot integration, academic focus

popular_solutions: Manual CSV downloads for PredictIt historical data
Bitquery GraphQL for on-chain Polymarket data
controversial_topics: Prediction markets vs sports betting legal classification
State gambling laws vs federal CFTC jurisdiction
College sports integrity concerns (NCAA letter to Kalshi)
Transfer portal betting ethics and legality
expert_opinions: Prediction markets offer better pricing (lower vig) than traditional sportsbooks
Sharp bettors migrating to prediction markets for CLV advantages
Legal uncertainty may persist until US Supreme Court (2027+)
Polymarket's crypto settlement creates friction but avoids state banking regulations
Kalshi's CFTC approval provides regulatory moat but slower market creation
